Cromwell workflow report

rMAP-GWAS

Reproducible microbial GWAS from paired-end reads, including gene presence/absence GWAS, optional SNP marker GWAS, population-structure visualization, post-GWAS reference annotation, and ranked association reporting.

CromwellDockerizedDistance-corrected pyseerMulti-pathogen readyGenBank annotation rescueOptional SNP GWAS
Phenotype legendPipelinePopulation structureGene plotsSNP GWASPriority hitsReference annotationValidationOutputs
Smoke-test/sample-size note: This run has 76 samples (15 cases (HIV positive) and 61 controls (HIV negative)). It is useful for workflow validation, but association results should not be treated as final biological or clinical findings without larger cohorts and independent validation.
cases (HIV positive)
15
controls (HIV negative)
61
Total samples
76
Top gene hit
PE_PGRS22-like (group_1618)
group_1618 | Rv1091 | low confidence | identity=99.88% | coverage=58.55%

01 Phenotype legend and coding

Phenotype tested: Binary phenotype hiv_status. The report displays the metadata-derived biological contrast throughout the HTML: case (HIV positive) = 1 and control (HIV negative) = 0.
ClassPyseer codingMetadata columnMetadata value used for displaySamplesReport label
case1hiv_statusHIV positive15case (HIV positive)
control0hiv_statusHIV negative61control (HIV negative)

02 Workflow architecture

01
Input
Sample-set validation

Checks sample names, paired FASTQs, group labels, and case (HIV positive)/control (HIV negative) balance.

02
QC
fastp trimming

Generates cleaned reads plus QC summaries.

03
Assembly
Shovill

Builds de novo genome assemblies using safe Cromwell memory handling.

04
Annotation
Prokka + Panaroo

Creates GFF annotations and pangenome gene matrices.

05
GWAS
Mash + pyseer

Runs population-structure-aware gene and optional SNP association testing.

06
Rescue
GenBank annotation

Maps prioritized pangenome/SNP markers to reference GenBank features where possible.

03 Run configuration

Reference name
MTBC_2026_06
Species
Mycobacterium tuberculosis complex
Reference Docker
gmboowa/rmap-gwas-mtbc-refs:2026.06
Generated UTC
2026-06-16T21:21:58Z
Phenotype coding: case (HIV positive) = 1; control (HIV negative) = 0. GWAS mode: gene_presence_absence_plus_snp; SNP branch: run; container backend recorded as docker.

04 Top-hit GenBank annotation rescue

Display name
PE_PGRS22-like (group_1618)
Pangenome/SNP marker
group_1618
Reference locus / gene
Rv1091 / PE_PGRS22
Confidence
low
Top-hit interpretation: Low-confidence GenBank rescue; treat as tentative and keep the Panaroo cluster ID. Reference identity: 99.88%; reference coverage: 58.55%. Product: PE-PGRS family protein PE_PGRS22.

05 Population structure

Interpretation check: Review case (HIV positive)/control (HIV negative) clustering before interpreting top hits. Strong phenotype-lineage clustering can indicate lineage-associated markers rather than causal phenotype-associated variation.

06 Gene presence/absence GWAS plots

Plot note: The gene association plot is a feature-index GWAS plot, not a full reference-coordinate Manhattan plot. Significant points are interpreted against the case (HIV positive) versus control (HIV negative) phenotype.
metric	value
pvalues_detected	444
points_drawn	444
plot_label	Gene presence/absence GWAS
significant_points_at_alpha	8
manhattan_type	feature_index_not_genomic_coordinate
qq_plot	generated

06 SNP marker GWAS

SNP branch: run. SNP marker GWAS is intended for mutation-mediated phenotypes, including MTBC drug-resistance traits. Review case (HIV positive)/control (HIV negative) population structure before interpreting SNP hits.
metric	value
plot_label	SNP marker GWAS
pvalues_detected	5676
significant_points_at_alpha	1
points_drawn	5000
manhattan_type	reference_coordinate_when_available
qq_plot	generated

09 Reference annotation confidence guide

Annotation caveat: Reference annotation rescue is intended to improve interpretability of pangenome/SNP markers. Low-confidence matches should not be treated as definitive gene calls. Candidate hits should be validated in independent datasets and interpreted in the context of case (HIV positive) versus control (HIV negative).
ConfidenceRuleInterpretation
high>=95% identity and >=90% coverage, or exact qualifier-level supportStrong reference-supported annotation
medium>=85% identity and >=70% coveragePlausible annotation; inspect manually
low>=60% identity and >=50% coverage, or weak/partial supportTentative annotation only
noneNo usable GenBank matchKeep the pangenome/SNP marker identifier

method	GenBank qualifier matching plus pure-Python nucleotide similarity rescue when Panaroo representative sequences are available

- Recommended minimum for stable microbial GWAS is often >=50 cases (HIV positive) and >=50 controls (HIV negative).

Status: PASS

11 Panaroo summary

12 Interpretation guidance

Microbial GWAS can be confounded by lineage structure, outbreak clustering, recombination, phenotype misclassification, and small sample size. Interpret candidate markers as associations with case (HIV positive) versus control (HIV negative), not as proven causal determinants.

13 Key output files

Phenotype
rMAP_GWAS_phenotype_legend.tsv

Metadata-derived legend used throughout this report.

GWAS
pyseer_gene_assoc.tsv

Raw pyseer gene association results. Rows detected: 444.

SNP GWAS
pyseer_snp_assoc.tsv

Raw pyseer SNP marker association results. Rows detected: 5676.

Priority
rMAP_GWAS_top_priority_hits.annotated.tsv

Ranked top gene hits with reference annotation columns.

Structure
mash_distances.tsv

Square Mash distance matrix used for correction and visualization.

Report
rMAP_GWAS_report.html

Integrated HTML report with phenotype legend, GWAS plots, annotation, and provenance summaries.

rMAP-GWAS report generated from successful Cromwell workflow outputs.
